name: Pratie Cakes
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Potatoes (mashed, fresh or prepared from flakes)
recipeInstructions:
1. Transfer the mashed potatoes to a large bowl.
2. Add the flour, salt, and onion.
3. Mix thoroughly with hands until fully combined and smooth.
4. Pat the mixture onto a lightly floured board until about 1/2 inch thick.
5. Cut into rounds using a 3-inch cookie cutter.
6. Place on a cookie sheet covered with waxed paper.
7. Chill in the refrigerator until ready to cook.
8. Heat 1 tablespoon margarine in a large frying pan or stovetop griddle.
9. Fry cakes over moderately hot heat, turning them to brown on both sides.
10. Serve immediately.
Food exchange per serving: 1 STARCH EXCHANGE + 1 FAT EXCHANGE
CHO: 15g; PRO: 2g; FAT: 5g; CAL: 110
Low-sodium diets: Omit salt from the mashed potato mixture and recipe.
Certain Irish folk refer to potato cakes as "praties". They are great!
Mix and shape ahead, then chill before cooking.
